questions.question: A shipping company has $25$ trucks, and $15%$ of them are currently in maintenance. How many trucks are in maintenance?
The traffic flow on a highway is $45%$ more than it was last year. If there were $200$ cars last year, how many are there now?
A delivery company reduces its delivery time by $12%$. If the original delivery time was $50$ minutes, what is the new delivery time?
A logistics company has a $25%$ increase in shipping costs. If the original cost was $$1000$, what is the new cost?
The number of flights at an airport increases by $18%$ from $250$ flights. How many flights are there now?
A transportation company reduces its workforce by $8%$. If there were originally $400$ employees, how many are left?
A package delivery company increases its delivery rate by $22%$. If the original delivery rate was $120$ packages per hour, what is the new rate?
The cost of fuel for a truck increases by $5%$ from $$2.50$ per gallon. What is the new cost per gallon?

A shipping company has two types of trucks: large and small. The large trucks have a $15%$ increase in maintenance costs, while the small trucks have a $20%$ decrease. If the large trucks originally cost $$5000$ to maintain and the small trucks cost $$3000$, what is the total change in maintenance costs if there are $10$ large trucks and $15$ small trucks?

A logistics company is considering two shipping options. Option A has a $10%$ increase in shipping time, while Option B has a $12%$ decrease in shipping costs. If the original shipping time is $30$ hours and the original cost is $$1000$, which option is more cost-effective in terms of $$ per hour$?

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of calculating percentages correctly
Use real-world examples to illustrate percent increase and decrease
Encourage students to check their answers for reasonableness
